sample_rows.Unnamed: 37: Pre Process and Classroom Observation Rubrics

sample_rows.Unnamed: 38: Rarely communicates or engages with peers or trainers. Shows resistance to feedback and lacks teamwork.
sample_rows.Unnamed: 39: Communicates inconsistently. Struggles with collaboration or adapting to group dynamics. Accepts feedback but rarely applies it.
sample_rows.Unnamed: 40: Communicates clearly and respectfully. Works well with others and applies feedback constructively.
sample_rows.Unnamed: 41: Proactively communicates and collaborates. Builds positive relationships and consistently applies feedback to improve.
sample_rows.Unnamed: 55: Poor communication, disengaged, resistant to feedback, and negatively affects team dynamics.
sample_rows.Unnamed: 56: Inconsistent communication or collaboration. Accepts feedback but applies it irregularly.
sample_rows.Unnamed: 57: Communicates clearly, collaborates well, and applies feedback constructively.
sample_rows.Unnamed: 58: Proactively communicates, uplifts team morale, and consistently applies feedback to improve.

sample_rows.Unnamed: 38: Unable to grasp basic concepts. Frequently makes errors and requires constant assistance.
sample_rows.Unnamed: 39: Understands basic concepts but struggles with application. Needs frequent guidance.
sample_rows.Unnamed: 40: Demonstrates understanding of key concepts. Applies skills with minimal supervision.
sample_rows.Unnamed: 41: Shows strong grasp of technical concepts. Applies skills independently and helps peers when needed.
sample_rows.Unnamed: 55: Frequent unexcused absences or tardiness. Affects team and training flow.
sample_rows.Unnamed: 56: Occasional attendance issues. Shows effort to improve.
sample_rows.Unnamed: 57: Consistently present and punctual. Follows attendance policies.
sample_rows.Unnamed: 58: Perfect or near-perfect attendance. Reliable and committed.

sample_rows.Unnamed: 38: Frequently absent or late without valid reason. Disrupts training flow.
sample_rows.Unnamed: 39: Occasionally late or absent. Shows some effort to improve attendance.
sample_rows.Unnamed: 40: Regularly present and punctual. Follows attendance guidelines.
sample_rows.Unnamed: 41: Perfect or near-perfect attendance. Always punctual and dependable.
sample_rows.Unnamed: 55: Disrespectful, disruptive, or non-compliant with company policies.
sample_rows.Unnamed: 56: Occasionally unprofessional or disengaged. Needs reminders.
sample_rows.Unnamed: 57: Professional, respectful, and follows conduct guidelines.
sample_rows.Unnamed: 58: Role model behavior. Positively influences peers and upholds values.

sample_rows.Unnamed: 38: Displays disrespectful or disruptive behavior. Ignores rules and expectations.
sample_rows.Unnamed: 39: Occasionally unprofessional or disengaged. Needs reminders to follow conduct guidelines.
sample_rows.Unnamed: 40: Maintains respectful and professional behavior. Follows rules and contributes positively.
sample_rows.Unnamed: 41: Exemplifies ideal trainee behavior. Encourages others and upholds training values consistently.
sample_rows.Unnamed: 55: Unable to navigate basic system functions. Frequently confused or lost.
sample_rows.Unnamed: 56: Understands some features but struggles with efficiency or accuracy.
sample_rows.Unnamed: 57: Navigates systems confidently with minimal errors.
sample_rows.Unnamed: 58: Efficient and independent system use. Supports peers and uses advanced features.

sample_rows.Unnamed: 38: Unable to navigate basic system functions. Frequently gets lost or confused during tasks.
sample_rows.Unnamed: 39: Understands some system features but struggles with efficiency or accuracy. Needs frequent support.
sample_rows.Unnamed: 40: Navigates systems confidently and completes tasks with minimal errors. Requires little supervision.
sample_rows.Unnamed: 41: Expertly navigates systems. Uses shortcuts, helps others, and completes tasks efficiently.
sample_rows.Unnamed: 55: Lacks understanding of product features and benefits.
sample_rows.Unnamed: 56: Basic product knowledge. Struggles to explain or apply it.
sample_rows.Unnamed: 57: Solid product understanding. Can explain and apply features effectively.
sample_rows.Unnamed: 58: Deep product knowledge. Anticipates needs and supports others confidently.

sample_rows.Unnamed: 38: Shows little understanding of the product. Unable to explain features or answer basic questions.
sample_rows.Unnamed: 39: Has partial understanding of the product. Can answer some questions but lacks depth.
sample_rows.Unnamed: 40: Demonstrates solid product knowledge. Can explain features and answer most questions accurately.
sample_rows.Unnamed: 41: Deep understanding of the product. Anticipates questions, explains clearly, and supports peers confidently.
sample_rows.Unnamed: 55: Fails to meet quality standards. Frequent critical errors.
sample_rows.Unnamed: 56: Meets some quality standards but inconsistently.
sample_rows.Unnamed: 57: Consistently meets quality benchmarks with minimal errors.
sample_rows.Unnamed: 58: Exceeds quality standards. Delivers high accuracy and attention to detail.

sample_rows.Unnamed: 20: What is a Progress Report? A batch report is a means to provide feedback on a training group and on individual trainees.
sample_rows.Unnamed: 55: Significantly above target AHT. Inefficient handling of tasks.
sample_rows.Unnamed: 56: Slightly above target AHT. Needs to improve speed and flow.
sample_rows.Unnamed: 57: Within target AHT range. Handles tasks efficiently.
sample_rows.Unnamed: 58: Below target AHT without compromising quality. Highly efficient.

sample_rows.Unnamed: 20: Purpose: This is to help provide extra support to those who need it most, and allow the transition leads, team leads and QA to best work with their new agents
sample_rows.Unnamed: 55: Excessive hold time. Poor communication or system navigation.
sample_rows.Unnamed: 56: Hold time slightly above acceptable range. Needs improvement in multitasking.
sample_rows.Unnamed: 57: Maintains acceptable hold time. Communicates hold reasons clearly.
sample_rows.Unnamed: 58: Minimal hold time. Manages tasks and communication seamlessly.

sample_rows.Unnamed: 55: Frequently out of schedule adherence. Disrupts workflow.
sample_rows.Unnamed: 57: Follows schedule consistently. Adheres to breaks and log-in times.
sample_rows.Unnamed: 58: Perfect adherence. Demonstrates strong time management and discipline.

sample_rows.Unnamed: 10: Class Summary - Provides a consolidated overview of key metrics such as attendance, attrition, and Nesting Quality Score for quick reference

sample_rows.Unnamed: 1: Update “Daily Observations” in Progress Report to track agent participation, strengths and opportunities for improvement, as well as performance related conversations (good or bad)
Daily call when class is behind schedule
Complete reviews of previous day each morning
Conduct meetings at the beginning and end of each day todiscuss updates, tips and tricks, concerns, questions, etc
Update Progress Report daily, specifically the "Nesting QA", Daily Observations and Attendance Tab
Ensure trainees are tapping the swipe clock if applicable
Ensure they are logging into SoftPhone or IceBar at the very start of the day.
Answer questions in a floor support capacity
Work with Ops to ensure floor support is readily available to assist with nesting
Coach back QAs (or vendor response if applicable) as they arrive

columns: Select the name of your primary trainer for this course.
Sum of Sum of User Score
Select your location from the drop-down menu.
What is your training start date?
Select the name of your primary trainer for this course..1
If there was a secondary trainer assisting your class, then please select their name or select None.
It was easy for me to get actively involved during this program.
I was comfortable with the pace of this program.
I had many opportunities to get answers to my questions.
I had opportunities to practice the skills I was asked to learn.
My learning was enhanced by the knowledge of the facilitator.
My learning was enhanced by the experiences shared by the facilitator.
What are three things the facilitator did well that encouraged you?
What are three things that the facilitator could do differently next time?

sample_rows.name: Guest
sample_rows.What are three things the facilitator di: she was available to help , active listening, patient with us
sample_rows.What are three things that the facilitat: nothing to add every thing was perfect for the training.

sample_rows.name: Guest
sample_rows.What are three things the facilitator di: Ankit made sure he was always available for our questions, always willing and ready to explain until everyone understood, also when answering our queries he made sure to explain with detail so that we go back to the cardholder and explain in clear terms
sample_rows.What are three things that the facilitat: Everything Ankit did was Great, the only thing that i was bummed about it that not everyone was willing to participate and speak on voice, that makes the training so much more interesting

sample_rows.name: Guest
sample_rows.What are three things the facilitator di: Always ask probing questions, always check SOP and always thank the Card holder

sample_rows.name: Guest
sample_rows.What are three things the facilitator di: Ankit always do a recap each day regarding the first time calls we encountered. When one of us asked for his help, he make sure that all of us will be able to know that new knowledge as well, so that if where going to encounter it we already have a prior knowledge about that concern. He always takes time to asked us if we\'re able to get the self led module.

sample_rows.name: Guest
sample_rows.What are three things the facilitator di: They\'re lways remind me to use the sop, always be mindful in terms of validation & must do the call flow
sample_rows.What are three things that the facilitat: I think for the future was nothing I can\'t tell right now because they\'re gave more information that we need in diffirent scenario in terms of skills, tools and the flow

sample_rows.name: Guest
sample_rows.What are three things the facilitator di: considerate, having long patience, answering accurately the questions i had

sample_rows.name: Guest
sample_rows.What are three things the facilitator di: by explaining how we can handle the call, giving appropriate examples to provide resolution and teaching the concerns that might be encountered.

sample_rows.name: Guest
sample_rows.What are three things the facilitator di: For me, I had a hard time to find the right SOP for the given concern. \\nHowever, Ate Sheena, told us that we really need to allot time in order for me to understand the SOP easily and also to give the best solution to the given problem. \\n\\nThe second thing was, we always from our mistakes. During the second day of the nesting week, the pressure was lessen and so far I was able to enjoy the call because of how kind the customer is. \\n\\nLastly, the floor support was very hands on even though there are some chances that all of us need them, but then they can still manage their time to rotate from one another to help and cater the concern of the card holder.
sample_rows.What are three things that the facilitat: -able to have another floor support \\n-it is way more enjoyable if we can meet the trainer because it is totally different to have a face to face training\\n-I don\'t think that there\'s still a need to improve or give the facilitator an advise because without them, trainees are nothing. \\n\\n\\nTHANK YOU SO MUCH, SCOTT & MS. CATHE! :)

sample_rows.name: Guest
sample_rows.What are three things the facilitator di: Mr Scoot and Miss Catherine is very approachable  when asking a question  also they very generosity
sample_rows.What are three things that the facilitat: none so far Mr scoot and Miss Catherine is very professional during our training period

sample_rows.name: Guest
sample_rows.What are three things the facilitator di: Patience on discussing, Kindness that we can see every single day of training, Passion that we can feel through the whole training days. :)
sample_rows.What are three things that the facilitat: Nothing, the facilitator did a good job on educating us 8) :)

sample_rows.name: Guest
sample_rows.What are three things the facilitator di: THEY BOOST OUR CONFIDENT ESP US NEWBIE, GUIDE US WELL TO ENGAGED WELL TO THE ENVIRONMENT, HAVING MORE PATIENCE

sample_rows.name: Guest
sample_rows.What are three things the facilitator di: Motivates me, Trained me well, and encourage me

sample_rows.name: Guest
sample_rows.What are three things the facilitator di: Helping us to answer our question during training

sample_rows.name: Guest
sample_rows.What are three things the facilitator di: the facilitator did well that encourage me would be process and procedure., activate listening

sample_rows.name: Guest
sample_rows.What are three things the facilitator di: provide all information for this account\\n

sample_rows.name: Guest
sample_rows.What are three things the facilitator di: Their positivity towards success, their encouragement, and  willingness to share their knowledge.
sample_rows.What are three things that the facilitat: All is well. They are doing great.

sample_rows.Unnamed: 1: Paperwork and tickets not related to class prep, emails and reports out side of class hours, PR updates outside of class hours.
All activities related to setting up a class, inclduing but not limited to tickets, WebEx, PR set up, ECN's, CMPs and following up on equipment.
Time spent facilitating a class, excluding breaks and lunches.
Time spent assisting a Facilitator in class, this includes tickets, emails and reports during class hours.
Answering questions, reviewing reports, QA Coachbacks, commincations with Ops regarding nesters.
Coaching agents of outside of class hours, this includes staying to answer questions during breaks, lunch or after class.
Team meetings, 1:1 with TM, Hand-off meetings and other meetings with Ops
Reviewing content for accuracy and submitting change log requests out side of training hours.
Workshops or courses during business hours including LinkedIn Learning
Vacation and approved leaves including breavement (code as 7.5 hours)
As assigned by your TM, examples include, reworking job aids, reports on behalf of a TM, implementing process improvements and so on.
Assist QA team with quotas when not in training
ATs when not in training or TSs if working production overtime.
Support or observations of other departments such as Recritment, Design and WFM.
Assignments to support the Training Manager such as peer-to-peer observations, reports and special meetings.

sample_rows.Learning and Development Observation Wor: 1. Use this form to observe a specialist/agent trainer.

3. Provide specific examples under strengths and challenges.
4. After you observe, complete the one-page Trainer Summary at the end of this document.
5. Conduct a post instructor feedback session using GROW coaching model within 24 hours of the observation.
